question: Does the observed CSI gain come from static geography, an event-level scalar moisture regime, or event-specific spatial PWV anomalies?

decomposition.static_climatology: fit-day spatial mean PWV field repeated through the seven-anchor history
decomposition.event_scalar: per-anchor valid-domain mean departure from fit-day climatology
decomposition.event_spatial_anomaly: residual after removing static climatology and event scalar, constrained to zero valid-domain spatial mean

same_checkpoint_variants: event scalar only without static geography
event spatial anomaly only without static geography or scalar regime

primary_test.comparison: real causal PWV minus static climatology plus event scalar
primary_test.meaning: incremental contribution of event-specific spatial anomalies
primary_test.pass: CSI delta at least 0.003 and positive average-precision delta in at least three of four all-window horizon-threshold tasks

note: All variants use one trained checkpoint and one set of frozen probability thresholds.
